seoTitle: How to Make 4 Flyers on One Page in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to lay out four flyers on a single printable page in Canva to save paper and print materials more efficiently.
intro: Printing multiple small flyers on one page saves paper and print costs, but getting the layout right takes some planning. This tutorial walks through selecting the right flyer size, using Canva's grid tools, and arranging four flyers cleanly on one page.

steps.heading: Select the right flyer size
steps.body: Choose a flyer dimension that divides evenly into your printable page size, such as a quarter-page layout.

steps.heading: Use grids for alignment
steps.body: Turn on Canva's grid and guideline tools to keep each of the four flyers aligned and evenly spaced.

steps.heading: Customize your flyer designs
steps.body: Design each flyer section with the content, branding, and visuals you need for your promotion or event.

steps.heading: Print 4 flyers on one page
steps.body: Export the finished layout so all four flyers print together on a single sheet, ready to be cut apart.

faq.question: Do all four flyers need to have the same design?
faq.answer: No, you can design each of the four sections differently if you want variety, or repeat the same design for consistency.

faq.question: Will I need to cut the page myself after printing?
faq.answer: Yes, since the flyers are laid out on one page, you'll need to cut along the guides to separate them after printing.

faq.question: Can I use this layout technique for other print materials besides flyers?
faq.answer: Yes, the same grid and alignment approach works for other small print items like postcards or business cards.

faq.question: Does this save money on printing costs?
faq.answer: Yes, printing multiple flyers on a single sheet reduces the number of pages needed, which can lower printing costs.
